context: A Spanish bar or cafe is where half of daily life happens. You can often stand right at the bar, order in a few words, and pay when you leave - no need to wait to be seated or to flag anyone down.
explanation: Ordering is just "quiero" (I would like) plus what you want, finished with "por favor": "Un cafe, por favor". You don't have to build a full sentence - the drink alone, said politely, is enough.

examples.es: Un cafe con leche, por favor.

examples.note: Ask for it when you're ready - it rarely comes on its own.
